prose:
Frontier-orbital single-point calculation on formaldehyde (H₂C=O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) using DFT at tier 'standard' — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fit RI on by default) in PySCF. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral closed-shell singlet). Geometry: user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz, not re-relaxed (single-point only). SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings reported by the engine. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true.

Results:
- HOMO energy: −7.6523067 eV (orbital index 7, occupation 2.0)
- LUMO energy: −1.7826355 eV (orbital index 8, occupation 0.0)
- HOMO–LUMO gap: 5.8696712 eV (headline result)
- Total electronic energy: −114.55325 Hartree / −3117.1528 eV
- 8 occupied orbitals, 74 total orbitals

Koopmans-theorem descriptors: vertical IP = 7.6523067 eV, vertical EA = 1.7826355 eV, electronegativity = 4.7174711 eV, chemical hardness = 2.9348356 eV, softness = 0.34073459 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 3.7914447 eV.

CLI invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz
